pairs.a_text: Alex Hormozi asserts that if your goal (your why) is large enough and tied to something beyond self-interest, you can tolerate suffering that would otherwise exceed your personal threshold. He cites Viktor Frankl's observation that a big enough why enables endurance of almost any how.
pairs.b_text: Alex Hormozi defines fortitude as the magnitude of behavior change once a person’s tolerance threshold is exceeded. Low fortitude produces a large, steep drop in behavior quality (e.g., quitting a job, major life disruptions); high fortitude produces only a small, imperceptible shift before the person regains normal behavior.

pairs.b_text: Alex Hormozi defines tolerance as the quantity or duration of hardship a person can endure before their behavior changes from their intended baseline.
